steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The noun “pages” is directly followed by a prepositional phrase “a collection of Norse tales dating to 1270” that modifies it, creating a noun phrase that serves as the subject of the sentence.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A prepositional phrase that modifies a noun should not be separated from it by punctuation, so the correct answer has no punctuation after “from.” A colon, comma, or em dash would all incorrectly separate the prepositional phrase from the noun it modifies. That matches choice C — C is correct.
